title: Non-record: Negative results — quantization algorithms, TTT, architecture, and self-generated GPTQ calibration study
body:
## Summary

11 experiments on the 1.1142 BPB stack (GPTQ + XSA-all + BigramHash 3072×112), all negative or neutral. Plus a self-generated GPTQ calibration study quantifying the real-text vs random-noise gap at 0.0020 BPB.

---

## Self-Generated GPTQ Calibration Study

GPTQ requires calibration data to estimate H = X^T X per layer. We replaced val data with random tokens to measure how much real text helps.

Trained once (seed 314, 6,942 steps), saved checkpoint, tested 5 calibration configs on the same weights:

| # | Calibration Source | Tokens | Time | Sliding BPB | vs Val-calib |
|---|-------------------|--------|------|-------------|-------------|
| 1 | **Val data** | ~50M | ~5s | **1.11446** | — |
| 2 | Random tokens (64×1) | 131K | 3.4s | **1.11650** | +0.00204 |
| 3 | Random tokens (64×48) | 6.3M | 9.6s | **1.11657** | +0.00211 |
| 4 | Random tokens (256×48) | 25M | 35s | **1.11650** | +0.00204 |
| 5 | Gibbs-refined (3 rounds, 64×48) | 6.3M | 24.4s | **1.11663** | +0.00217 |

**Findings:**

1. **The gap is 0.0020 BPB — real text vs random noise.** This is not val-specific: [PR #772](https://github.com/openai/parameter-golf/pull/772) proved val and train are distributionally identical. Train data would produce the same Hessians and the same 0.0020 improvement. The gap reflects natural language activation covariance vs random-integer activation covariance.

2. **More random tokens don't help.** 131K and 25M tokens give identical BPB (1.11650). The Hessian converges with few samples because at int6 it mainly identifies dead columns and relative importance — properties of model architecture, not input statistics.

3. **Gibbs refinement doesn't help.** 3 rounds of forward → sample → replace produces slightly worse Hessians than plain random (1.11663 vs 1.11650).

4. **Self-generated at 1.1165 beats SOTA** (PR #549, 1.1194) by 0.003 BPB with zero legality risk.

**Why random tokens work at int6:** The Hessian diagonal (which drives column permutation) and off-diagonal structure (which guides error compensation) are dominated by the model's learned weights — embedding geometry, attention patterns, MLP scales — not by input token identity. At 63 grid levels, the rounding decisions are coarse enough that the Hessian quality threshold is low.

---

## Quantization Algorithm Experiments

Quant gap: +0.0036 BPB (pre-quant 1.1341 → roundtrip 1.1377). At int6, GPTQ is near-optimal.

| Technique | Paper | Result | Mechanism |
|-----------|-------|--------|-----------|
| **Qronos** (ICLR 2026) | arXiv:2505.11695 | +0.0007 ❌ | Re-collects Hessians from quantized activations. At int6, activation mismatch <0.1% — updated Hessians nearly identical. |
| **CDQuant** | arXiv:2406.17542 | +0.0005 ❌ | Coordinate descent re-visiting columns. At ~0.06 scale-unit spacing, most weights already at optimal grid point. |

## TTT Experiments (Score-First, Legal)

Same protocol as merged PR #549. 25 total TTT experiments have failed across two stacks.

| Approach | Params Unfrozen | TTT BPB | Baseline | Delta |
|----------|-----------------|---------|----------|-------|
| **Full TTT** | 27.1M (100%) | 1.1146 | 1.1139 | +0.0007 ❌ |
| **MLP-down** | 8.7M (32%) | 1.1145 | 1.1144 | +0.0001 |
| **MLP-all** | 17.3M (64%) | 1.1144 | 1.1144 | +0.0000 |

SGD lr=0.002, momentum=0.9, 3 epochs, 32K chunks, cosine LR, grad_clip=1.0.

**Why TTT fails here but worked on PR #549 (−0.0025 BPB):**
- XSA-all already captures inter-document context patterns that TTT was adapting to
- At 27M params, score-first TTT can't overcome the forgetting/adaptation tradeoff — early chunks get no benefit, and the model is too small for late-chunk gains to compensate
- The int6 GPTQ rounding decisions, optimized for real-text activations, are degraded when TTT shifts the weight distribution

## Architecture and Eval-Time Experiments

| Technique | Result | Mechanism |
|-----------|--------|-----------|
| **Spectral Init** (λ=10 on QKV, arXiv:2603.07162) | 1.52 BPB, 650ms/step ❌ | λ=10 is 200× Xavier init magnitude at 27M params. 924 steps in 600s vs 6,950 baseline. Paper tested ~100M models. |
| **SLOT bias** (512-dim additive, 3 AdamW steps/chunk) | +0.0013 ❌ | Global shift can't capture per-document patterns. Final-norm → logit pipeline already calibrated. |

---

## What's Exhausted

- **Quant algorithm:** Qronos, CDQuant both negative
- **Eval-time adaptation:** 3× TTT + SLOT all negative
- **Architecture:** Spectral Init catastrophic; Gated Attention (+0.0026, PR #609), DiffTransformer (1.5× slower, PR #418), Attention Residuals (54% slower, PR #362) all dead

**Untested:** Non-uniform quantization grid, rate-distortion quantization (CERWU), QK-Norm, Peri-LN.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
